consensus_findings.description: design.md:215 says in-process callers without dispatch metadata write 'quorum_requested: <N vendors>, quorum_received: <N vendors>' — but `quorum_received` should reflect actual SUCCESSFUL vendors, not the requested count. If 3 vendors were requested and 1 timed out, `quorum_received` should be 2, not 3. The current text would mislead operators reading the manifest.

R3.S3 (synthesis_failed defaults to False on happy path) is testable only if a ConvergenceResult is constructed. But the design says when synthesis raises, the exception propagates and a result is NOT returned. So the only way `synthesis_failed=True` can be observed is if a caller wraps converge() in try/except and constructs their own partial result. Spec doesn't say which callers do this. The field is essentially documented but never set by converge() itself.

The audit event `convergence.synthesis_failed_with_checkpoint` is only emitted when synthesis fails WITH checkpoint present. If the checkpoint write itself fails (OSError/PermissionError before synthesis), there is NO audit event. Operators relying on `query_audit('convergence.synthesis_failed_with_checkpoint')` to detect chronic synthesis issues would miss the case where the system can't even checkpoint. This is a silent failure mode.

review-cache-layout.schema.json marks `dispatches`, `quorum_requested`, `quorum_received` as required AND `additionalProperties: false`. After this proposal lands, all manifests have these fields. But operators inspecting a manifest from BEFORE this proposal landed (e.g., archived `.review-cache/` from a prior run) would fail schema validation. The schema description says these manifests will be 'regenerated next time the dispatcher runs' but doesn't define what 'regenerated' means for an archived/read-only directory.

Task 1.3 says 'grep for any caller that reads the manifest or globs the output dir.' But this is a one-time grep at implementation time, not a regression-detecting test. If someone adds a new caller post-merge that violates the manifest contract, nothing catches it. The shared manifest format is a coordination boundary; consumers SHOULD be enforced via fixture-based tests, not grep.

Spec R5 (Path Safety) S2 rejects vendor names outside `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`. But the existing dispatcher's vendor names include `gpt-5-codex` and `claude_code` — both compliant. Are there any vendor adapter configs in agents.yaml or elsewhere that emit names with periods, dots, or version qualifiers? The constraint should be verified against the actual vendor name set used in production, not just the development examples.

`contracts/finding.schema.json:41` defines `criticality` as `low|medium|high|blocking`, but the existing review findings schema and consensus synthesizer use `critical`, not `blocking` (`openspec/schemas/review-findings.schema.json:19`, `skills/parallel-infrastructure/scripts/consensus_synthesizer.py:125`). This schema would reject valid existing `critical` findings and accept `blocking`, which the synthesizer ranks as the default lowest severity.

`tasks.md:48` says synthesis exceptions propagate with "no try/except wrapper around synthesis," but `tasks.md:58` requires emitting an audit event before re-raising the original synthesis exception. The implementation cannot satisfy audit-on-failure without catching the synthesis exception at least long enough to audit and re-raise.

The spec says `ConvergenceResult` fields let callers detect synthesis failure (`spec.md:86`), but also says no `ConvergenceResult` is returned on the typical failure path (`spec.md:90`, `design.md:243`). As written, `synthesis_failed=True` has no observable API path or task that proves a caller can receive it after `converge()` raises.

`contracts/README.md` still describes CLI subprocess fallback and old fallback audit events at lines 3, 11, 12, and 21, despite the revised proposal explicitly dropping automatic recovery. It also says both schemas declare `schema_version: 1` at line 25, but `finding.schema.json` has no `schema_version` field.

tasks.md:58 (Task 4.2) and spec.md:113 (R4) depend on coordination_bridge.try_emit_audit_event(), which does not exist in the codebase, nor does the agent-coordinator HTTP API expose a POST /audit/log endpoint for custom events.
tasks.md:25 (Phase 1.2) migrates review_dispatcher.py to the new manifest format, but the dispatcher lacks a change_id parameter required by contracts/review-cache-layout.schema.json.

spec.md:90 (R3) and design.md:243 define ConvergenceResult.synthesis_failed=True, but this field is unreachable in converge() because the synthesis exception is re-raised before the result can be returned.
contracts/review-cache-layout.schema.json:81 and contracts/README.md:11 contain stale references to 'fallback CLI' and 'fallback_recovered/failed' events which were dropped in this revision.

finding.schema.json requires review_type, target, and reviewer_vendor in per-vendor files, but existing ReviewOrchestrator does not inject these into ReviewResult.findings, risking contract violations if the helper doesn't handle wrapping.
